businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker transitioning into a 'Physical AI Platform' spanning robotaxis, humanoid robots, and energy storage
businessSummary.description: Tesla is rapidly transforming from an EV manufacturer into an integrated AI, robotics, and energy platform company. FY2025 revenue was $94.8B, marking its first-ever annual revenue decline (-2.9%), with net income plunging 46.5%. Closed at $405.94 (+3.44%) on 3/4, bouncing back from the prior day's decline. Berlin Gigafactory faces union pressure from IG Metall, and NHTSA FSD crash data submission deadline looms on 3/9. P/E 375.87x, Forward P/E 195.87x. Market cap $1.52T.
howTheyMakeMoney: Vehicle sales: ~73% of revenue ($69.5B). Model 3/Y core, Model S/X discontinuing Q2 2026
Energy business: ~14% of revenue ($12.8B, +27% YoY). Megapack utility-scale storage
Services/Other: ~13% of revenue ($12.5B). Supercharger network, insurance, maintenance
FSD subscription: $99/month autonomous driving software (subscription-only since Feb 2026)
keyProducts: Cybertruck (base AWD trim with massive demand, deliveries pushed to 2027)
Optimus (humanoid robot, Fremont factory conversion underway)
Megapack (utility-scale energy storage, Megapack 3 and Mega Block launching 2026)

keyMetrics.name: P/E Ratio (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme valuation reflecting AI/robotics/energy premium, but highly overvalued vs current earnings

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Reflects earnings recovery expectations but still very elevated

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: First-ever FY decline. EV competition intensifying with price cuts

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Sharp decline from 7.24% in FY2024. Price cuts and R&D expansion

keyMetrics.name: Debt/Equity
keyMetrics.interpretation: Very healthy. Net cash position of $29.3B

keyMetrics.name: ROE
keyMetrics.interpretation: Declined vs FY2024. Profitability recovery needed

growthDrivers.title: NHTSA FSD data deadline March 9 — regulatory crossroads
growthDrivers.description: Motley Fool: Tesla must submit FSD crash data to NHTSA by 3/9. Results could accelerate autonomous driving approval or trigger additional restrictions.

growthDrivers.title: German union IG Metall pushing for influence at Berlin Gigafactory
growthDrivers.description: Reuters: Germany's largest industrial union campaigning for breakthrough at Berlin Gigafactory works council elections.

growthDrivers.title: Cybertruck base AWD trim — explosive demand, deliveries pushed to 2027
growthDrivers.description: New base trim generating massive demand, pushing expected delivery timelines to 2027.

growthDrivers.title: France & Norway market share rebounds in February — European stabilization
growthDrivers.description: Reuters: Tesla regained market share in France and Norway in February, signaling stabilization after two years of European decline.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share leader, though overtaken globally by BYD. Vertical integration across software, manufacturing, energy and 8.2B FSD miles are key moats.

moats.description: 8.2 billion FSD miles. Overwhelmingly larger training dataset vs competitors.
In-house batteries (4680), custom chips (HW4), software (FSD), charging infra (Supercharger).
NACS is North American EV charging standard. GM, Ford, Rivian depend on Tesla Supercharger network.

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: 2025 global EV sales leader. Rapid European growth, US entry limited by tariffs

competitors.name: Rivian (RIVN)
competitors.detail: R2 SUV ($45,000) launching 2026 to compete directly with Model Y

competitors.name: Xiaomi
competitors.detail: YU7 SUV outselling Model Y 2:1 in China

analystOpinions.notableComment: Hold consensus among 33 analysts. Current price $405.94 already above consensus $396.15. Autonomous driving and robotics execution is the key divider.

risks.title: Valuation risk — P/E 375x
risks.description: Extreme overvaluation vs current earnings. Significant correction risk if AI/robotics narrative fails.

risks.title: NHTSA FSD regulatory risk
risks.description: 3/9 data submission results could halt autonomous driving program.

risks.title: Global EV competition intensifying
risks.description: BYD, Xiaomi, Rivian threatening on price competitiveness and product diversity.

risks.title: European labor issues
risks.description: IG Metall union influence at Berlin factory could increase costs.

risks.title: CEO risk
risks.description: Elon Musk's political statements and multi-company management affecting Tesla focus.

buyReasons.title: FCF rebounded to $6.22B (+73.7% YoY)
buyReasons.rationale: Cash generation improving despite earnings decline. Investment capacity secured.

buyReasons.title: European market share stabilization
buyReasons.rationale: France & Norway rebounds after two-year decline. Potential bottom formation.

buyReasons.title: Cybertruck AWD demand explosion
buyReasons.rationale: Waitlist extending to 2027. Truck market penetration creating new demand.

overallOpinion: Tesla is attempting a transition from EV maker to AI platform company, but P/E of 375x remains the biggest risk.
The 3/9 NHTSA FSD data deadline is the key near-term catalyst/risk. Regulatory outcome will determine stock direction.
European stabilization, Cybertruck demand explosion, and energy growth are positive, but core auto business decline (-2.9%) and earnings collapse (-46.5%) are concerning.
Trading below 50-day MA ($431) but rebounded above 200-day MA ($391). RSI 45 neutral. Technically in a $391-$431 range.
